tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: The assignment is almost done, so I can just finish it quickly and be free. Since nothing else matters right now, I'll ignore my fatigue and power through this last bit.
I missed the deadline despite being 95% done; I'll just submit whatever is there now to stop worrying about it.
I'm 97% done and still stressed, but finishing this last bit is the only priority right now.
I'm finally at 99% and just need to submit this to get it over with. My stress is high, but finishing the task matters more than feeling tired.

desires: Instantly submit the assignment to end the acute stress and stop the mental loop of 'almost done'
Avoid any further interaction or friction with the professor regarding the missed deadline
Get immediate relief from the physical exhaustion caused by pushing through fatigue
Procrastinate on the low-priority note tidying because it offers no immediate reward

goals.description: Click 'Submit' on the graded assignment immediately to lock in the grade and terminate the mental loop.
goals.rationale: My achievement axis (0.5) makes securing this grade the absolute priority, but my impulsive nature (short horizon) means I cannot tolerate the lingering state of 'almost done.' The stress is acute; I need the immediate dopamine hit of completion right now. Perfectionism or polishing is irrelevant compared to the urge to close the loop instantly.

goals.description: Shut down the laptop and leave the workspace without sending any apology email or explanation to the professor.
goals.rationale: My avoidant conflict style dictates that interacting with the professor about the late submission is a high-risk, low-reward scenario. Any attempt to explain feels like inviting friction. I will prioritize escaping the social demand entirely to minimize cognitive load, hoping silence prevents an awkward confrontation later.

goals.description: Ignore the note tidying task completely and go lie down to crash from exhaustion.
goals.rationale: The filler task has no deadline and offers no immediate reward, which fits my procrastination bias perfectly. My physical stamina is depleted, but my sleep discipline is low; I won't plan a rest routine, I just want to collapse immediately after submitting to escape the fatigue.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (Achievement) by finishing the submission loop instantly to kill stress. Avoids g2 conflict entirely by skipping any professor interaction, satisfying my impulsive need for immediate closure over long-term relationship maintenance.

goals.description: Force-finish the remaining 10% of the graded assignment and hit submit immediately to eliminate the stressor.
goals.rationale: Achievement value dominates; I need the grade and the relief of escaping this task. The deadline is in 28 minutes, so I must ignore my fatigue (impulsive/short-horizon) and push through to secure the outcome.

goals.description: Do not engage with teammates or professor regarding the workload or deadline.
goals.rationale: Avoidant trait; interacting now risks friction or delays that could jeopardize the submission. Better to isolate and finish alone than risk a social conflict over time management.

desires: Finish the assignment immediately to secure the grade and escape the task entirely
Ignore my fatigue and sleep debt to avoid any delay in submission
Avoid discussing the deadline or workload with anyone to prevent friction

My achievement drive (0.5) is screaming for the grade, and my impulsive nature ignores the fact that I'm already late to focus entirely on the immediate 'fix' before the window slams shut. I need to stop thinking about sleep debt or social fallout and just brute-force this last bit.

goals.description: Send a brief, non-apologetic confirmation email to the professor immediately after submission to avoid future friction.
goals.rationale: I am avoidant; I want to prevent the professor from emailing me later with a complaint. However, interacting now risks delaying the submission. I will only do this if g1 is marked complete. If not, I'll risk the silence to save time.

goals.description: Close the laptop and collapse into bed immediately after submission.
goals.rationale: My sleep discipline is low (3), but I have 2 hours of sleep debt. Once the stressor is gone, my body will demand rest. This is a short-horizon desire to recover stamina, but it's secondary to securing the academic win first.
